channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson And Mike Catalini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Court upholds Amanda Knox conviction for slandering former boss in roommate's murder
item.description: Italy's Supreme Court on Thursday afternoon upheld Amanda Knox's criminal conviction for slandering her former boss, Patrick Lumumba, nearly two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Purdue Pharma and owners to pay $7.4 billion in settlement of lawsuits over the toll of OxyContin
item.description: Members of the family who own OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma, and the company itself, agreed to pay up to $7.4 billion in a new settlement to lawsuits over the toll of the powerful prescription painkiller, the attorneys general from several states announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump order ending federal DEI programs leaves agencies and stakeholders on uncertain ground
item.description: From federal agencies to stakeholders who get federal dollars for special training, many are trying to process how President Donald Trump’s sweeping executive order putting a stop to diversity, equity and inclusion programs across the U.S. government will upend their work.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, AYANNA ALEXANDER and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order to declassify files on JFK, RFK and MLK Jr. assassinations
item.description: “Give that to RFK Jr.,” Trump said about the pen he used to sign the order declassifying info about the JFK, RFK and MLK assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN announced a restructuring Thursday that includes some 200 layoffs, an accelerated pivot to digital operations and new TV roles for personalities like Wolf Blitzer, Jake Tapper and Audie Cornish.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bourbon Street terror attack, Trump Tower Cybertruck explosion probe launched by Senate committee
item.description: The Senate Judiciary Committee is investigating the New Orleans terrorist attack that left 15 people dead, as well as the explosion outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado DNA analyst appears on forgery charges as validity of more than 500 cases in doubt
item.description: GOLDEN, Colo. (AP) — A former Colorado Bureau of Investigation DNA scientist is facing 102 criminal charges, including dozens of counts of forgery, after authorities said she cut corners and didn't follow standard testing protocols in hundreds of criminal cases.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE arrests nearly 500 in nationwide immigration crackdown that includes Illinois
item.description: Federal immigration agents conducted the first large-scale arrest operation under the Trump administration, resulting in nearly 500 arrests.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, WGN 9

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Mailman murder suspect with history of violence indicted in brazen lunch break attack
item.description: Jaia Cruz, 24, indicted in stabbing murder of US mailman Ray Hodge III on his lunch break at a Manhattan deli earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College enrollment now higher than pre-pandemic levels
item.description: Some of the biggest increases were in undergraduate certificate programs, with enrollments 28.5 percent above 2019 levels.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Greensboro neighborhood rescues pregnant dog in winter weather
item.description: “She just kept going door to door, up driveways, up on porches, and somebody noticed her and called the shelter,” John Reiland said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Fambro, NewsNation

item.title: One dog dies, another rescued in Texas animal cruelty case
item.description: "There were two dogs outside of Bartlett on a property in a chain link fence with no support. No housing out in the cold with the wind blowing, whipping up the ice and the snow into their pen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge to weigh genetic evidence and search warrants in University of Idaho quadruple murder case
item.description: Attorneys for a man charged with murder in connection with the killings of four University of Idaho students are asking a judge to throw out most of the evidence in the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, who was convicted of orchestrating his far-right extremist group’s Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Details have been released on how 33 children were treated in the Indiana home of reported abusers Sonja and Brian Stafford for approximately 13 years.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, Fox 5

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs sues man who claimed to have incriminating sex tapes, says they didn't exist
item.description: Combs, 55, has pleaded not guilty to sex trafficking charges lodged against him after his September arrest.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Arkansas officer arrested, charged with assault in beating of handcuffed inmate
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A former Arkansas police officer who was caught on video beating a handcuffed inmate in the back of his patrol car last year has been arrested and charged with aggravated assault.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont worked at the Pentagon during 9/11, family says
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent who was killed in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border was a military veteran who worked security duty at the Pentagon during the Sept. 11 attacks, his family said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon orders 1,500 troops to secure U.S.-Mexico border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will begin deploying as many as 1,500 active duty troops to help secure the southern border in the coming days, U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
